summary: This cluster is united by the investigation of bioactive compounds from marine macroalgae, with a strong emphasis on antioxidant properties and species discovery. Research on brown seaweeds from Korean waters, specifically *Sargassum ringgoldianum subsp. coreanum* and *Ecklonia cava*, identified and characterized the compounds loliolide and dieckol, respectively, demonstrating their protective effects against oxidative stress in cellular assays. A separate study expanded the taxonomic and geographic scope by describing a novel red algal species, *Erythrolobus australicus sp. nov.*, from Australian mangrove ecosystems, using molecular phylogeny and chemotaxonomy to identify key compounds like floridoside. Collectively, these papers highlight a focus on the chemical characterization and biological activity of algal metabolites, spanning both the Pacific and Australian regions.
